scenario.title: Deterministic fake source drives real Tower and Gooseweb observers
preconditions: A clean committed candidate is served under the sole migration lease.
Default URL contains no fixture query parameter.
The versioned p02-fake-gooselake/v1 source is configured through public runtime contracts.
The supervisor explicitly runs the p02-verification feature example with a separate secret-gated observer listener and VITE_GOOSEWEB_DEBUG_FRAMES=true.
Epoch reconstruction uses two supervisor-owned immutable attempts: stop and release epoch 001, then start a fresh source and Tower both configured for epoch 002.

expected_runtime: one p02-turn-001 completed turn record with the exact submitted text
one p02-event-0004 turn.completed event with P02 deterministic terminal text
expected_goosetower: the separate verification listener reports whether p02-session-001 materialized the retained source sequence 4 event
one real session_detail terminal-text patch is encoded by the gateway
a missing materialized event or served frame fails the attempt at the first directly observed layer without inferring a cause
expected_worker_store: production RealtimeWorkerCore consumes any gateway frame actually served
window.__GOOSEWEB_VERIFICATION_OBSERVER__.capture() reports the bounded redacted real frame/output/store sequence without inferring a missing upstream frame
sessionDetails[p02-session-001] contains the exact terminal text once
expected_visible: P02 deterministic terminal is visible once; any absence makes the attempt changes_required until fresh four-layer evidence localizes it
the submitted composer clears without displaying a phantom terminal success

expected_runtime: one new p02-message-command-001 record and p02-delivery-command-001 record
one matching team_message.created event with scoped sequence 2
expected_goosetower: the real team materializer contains p02-message-command-001 and its delivery
one matching team_workspace patch/frame preserves the stable message identity
expected_worker_store: the delivery remains linked to the same message ID
expected_visible: P02 deterministic broadcast action is visible once in Team Comms
the injected delivery state is visible without a phantom success

required: fixed session, turn, message, delivery, process, clock, IDs, epoch, and source sequences
delivery records for intended recipients only
bounded replay plus duplicate, gap, epoch, disconnect, delay, rejection, and terminal facts
unknown public fields retained and every observer value redacted at capture
real runtime client, SSE fan-in, materializer, and gateway path consume the public source
materialized state and decoded frames carry stable identity and explicit source/gateway cursors
the feature-gated example exposes bounded capture-time-redacted observations on a separate secret-gated listener while the default product binary and HTTP router remain unchanged
production decoder and Worker/store path consume deterministic captured frames
read-only observer reports exact normalized state without inferring unavailable evidence
the explicit development debug-frames build exposes a bounded capture-time-redacted window observer that is absent from production/default builds
exact deterministic turn and team text visible once after supervisor review

golden_goose_acceptance_lessons: no composer without a selected thread/team
live projection and window coverage honesty

gooselake.reason: The fake source simulates public Gooselake authority only on a separate verification listener; production runtime behavior is unchanged.

goosetower.reason: Real Tower runtime client, SSE, materializer, gateway, and frame paths are exercised by an explicit p02-verification feature example with a separate observer listener; the default binary, product router, and production materialization semantics are unchanged.

gooseweb.reason: The real protocol decoder, Worker, and external store are observed by verification scripts; no fake data or control is bundled into default or production UI paths.

auth_account_state.reason: No auth/account state changes; runtime bearer and verification-control values are never exposed by observers and all evidence is capture-time redacted.

protocol_generated_code.reason: No Protobuf schema or generated output is edited; captured frames use the existing generated decoder.

api_narrative_docs.reason: No product HTTP behavior or OpenAPI changes; fake-source controls and Tower observations exist only on separate secret-gated verification listeners.

assertions: composer and primary send action are inside viewport
focus, Escape, and return behavior are correct where a sheet is used

empty.expectation: Clear, non-fixture empty state and no stale rows.

loading.expectation: Honest loading state without fake message text.

stale_gap_replaying.expectation: Visible stale/replay state; no command is presented as converged.

rejection_conflict.expectation: Specific terminal rejection and no phantom visible success.

permission_denied.reason: P01 runs the existing owner/dev-auth flow; RBAC denial becomes product scope in P15-P17.

unsupported_provider.reason: Team delivery visibility is provider-independent in this current scenario.

terminal_error.expectation: Specific failure remains visible and cannot be classified as success.

default_fixture_leak_assertions: URL has no gooseweb fixture parameter.
Visible text contains no dev fixture markers or fake entities.
Fixture-only globals and controls are absent.
fixtures.gating: Any visual fixture must require both import.meta.env.DEV and an explicit query parameter.

baseline_detected.description: Runtime message/delivery/event and injection succeed while Team Comms remains empty.
Runtime terminal truth succeeds while the materialized/browser session remains turn_running.
The semantic Start lead control produces no runtime/materialized session, pending state, or visible terminal rejection, so the source-backed Team Comms precondition cannot be established.

baseline_detected.first_divergent_layer: Gooseweb Worker/store to React connection-state projection
baseline_detected.description: Offline/online and fresh-context reconstruction do not expose an honest connection state; the top level reports degraded while source health reports live.
After reload at 520x900, Team Comms is absent from exposed semantic controls, so the critical surface is unreachable.
P01 and agent-browser provide no redacted WebSocket frame observer, so frame behavior is recorded as unavailable and never inferred.
Every fresh browser attempt requests GET /favicon.ico and receives HTTP 404; the failed request remains blocked until the static asset route is corrected.

A deterministic source-sequence gap reaches real RuntimeSseFanIn, whose Live to GapDetected transition is rejected by the current source-health lifecycle algebra in debug builds. P02 detects and localizes the baseline without changing P06 product behavior.

baseline_detected.first_divergent_layer: Goosetower session-detail snapshot kind to Gooseweb decoder/store
baseline_detected.description: In a fresh epoch-002 stack, reset row 1 and its terminal assistant text traverse the real live path and render correctly, but ordinary reload reconstructs the same session as No messages yet because the session-detail snapshot kind is decoded/stored without terminal detail. P02 records the exact pre-existing reconstruction defect from attempt 5 without correcting or approving P08 product scope.

schema_migration.reason: P02 adds versioned verification fixtures only; no persistent product schema changes.

backfill.reason: No product data changes or historical transformation occur.

compatibility.reason: The fake source targets current public runtime contracts and existing protocol; no compatibility surface changes.

rollback.reason: Verification artifacts are removed by reverting the P02 commit; product state is untouched.

performance.reason: P05 owns product performance calibration; P02 fixtures cap batches, replay pages, records, and observer output.

security.reason: P02 observers redact bearer, ticket, credential, cookie, CSRF, secret, and raw-image values at capture and controls require a separate verification secret.

data_retention_gc.reason: P02 fixed in-memory source state is bounded and ephemeral; it creates no durable product objects.

deployment.reason: No production deployment change; the supervisor may run the separate verification source only under the migration lease.

provenance_reuse.reason: Golden Goose deterministic timeline and Team Comms tests were read as design references; no source code was copied or adapted.

native_tauri_assumptions_removed.reason: No Golden Goose implementation code was reused.
